name: the primitive type called dead

VINIT.justification: The raw geometric potential before any dimensionality is assigned.

TANCH.justification: The absolute limit of the 2D sheet which contains all local operations.

AFWD.justification: Movement across the flat surface toward a coordinate target.

AREV.justification: The reversal of a vector within the local plane.

CLINK.justification: The sequential application of linear maps on the sheet.

IMSCRIB.justification: The recognition of a specific coordinate as itself without depth.

FSPLIT.justification: A single trajectory on the sheet splitting into two distinct planar directions.

FFUSE.justification: The meeting of two planar trajectories back into a single coordinate point.

EVALT.justification: Confirmation that the operation remains within the 2D local manifold.

EVALF.justification: The failure state where a coordinate exceeds the planar boundary.

ENGAGR.justification: A state where a point occupies two planar locations simultaneously before collapse.

IFIX.justification: The permanent marking of a point on the sheet, creating an immutable record.

phase_3.void_description: unallocated memory before the 2D manifold is initialized
phase_3.true_description: successful mapping within the local 2D sheet
phase_3.both_description: ambiguous coordinate state held in the local register

steps.domain_action: identify the origin point on the 2D sheet
translate from origin to the first local coordinate
branch the trajectory into two parallel planar paths
confirm the primary path remains within the 2D boundary
extend the primary path toward the target
reverse the secondary path for parity check
detect if the secondary path approaches the boundary limit
reunite the paths at the destination coordinate
compose the total translation into a single affine map
fix the final coordinate in the permanent record
close the planar boundary and terminate the operation

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The operation is a reversible geometric translation within a closed system.
